assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observable outputs respond to legal input changes without assuming internal state.
Checks focus on input/output consistency rather than cycle-accurate timing.
Co is the carry-out of the 16-bit addition
cases.goal: Confirm basic functional mapping for random legal inputs.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Randomized stimulus covers typical usage across the full operand range.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known non-control inputs: ['a', 'b', 'Cin']
Randomly generate a, b, Cin values across their full ranges.
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.
Observe outputs after input stabilization; no explicit latency assumed.

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
cases.goal: Exercise boundary-value and width-sensitive input patterns.
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['a', 'b']
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.

expected_properties: Output y and Co reflect the most recent input vector after combinational propagation.
cases.goal: Validate that rapid successive input changes do not cause glitches beyond combinational propagation.
notes: Since the adder is combinational, outputs should settle to each new input without internal state.
preconditions: Inputs a, b, Cin are driven with valid binary values.
stimulus_intent: Apply two distinct input vectors back-to-back without intervening idle cycles.
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after each input vector stabilizes; no clock needed.

cases.defer_reason: Advanced derived cases were downgraded because the contract does not yet justify deterministic mainline semantics.
expected_properties: y and Co are identical when inputs a and b are swapped while Cin unchanged.
notes: Ensures functional correctness independent of operand ordering.
Advanced derived cases were downgraded because the contract does not yet justify deterministic mainline semantics.
preconditions: Same Cin value for both stimulus vectors.
stimulus_intent: Drive vector (a,b,Cin) then vector (b,a,Cin) and compare outputs.
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after each vector stabilizes.
